items.title: Apple raises iCloud+ subscription prices in several countries
items.summary: Following recent hardware price increases in the US and Japan, along with subscription hikes in the US and other markets, Apple has now raised iCloud+ prices in several countries. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple cites paused securities fraud case in bid to halt Epic proceedings
items.summary: Apple is citing a newly paused securities fraud lawsuit to support its request to pause further lower-court proceedings in its legal battle with Epic Games while the Supreme Court reviews part of the dispute. Here are the details.

items.title: Investigation reveals dozens of disguised gambling apps on the App Store in Brazil
items.summary: An investigation by 9to5Mac reveals dozens of apps that disguise gambling platforms as simple games and utilities. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple increases iPhone prices in Japan by up to 11%
items.summary: Apple has raised iPhone prices in Japan by up to 11% today. The changes affect the iPhone 17 lineup, iPhone Air, and iPhone 16e. Here are the exact increases and the likely explanation.

items.title: Apple ordered to remove 8 AI nonconsensual undressing apps from the App Store [U]
items.summary:
Update, 4:29 p.m. ET: Apple reached out to 9to5Mac with more information, which you can find below the original post.

San Francisco city attorney David Chiu has sent cease-and-desist letters to Apple and Google, demanding that they remove several “nudify” apps from their app stores. Here are the details.

items.title: 9to5Mac Overtime 073: iOS 27 Public Beta
items.summary:
Jeff and Fernando discuss the iOS 27 Public Beta, some of their favorite new iOS 27 new features, and whether or not people should consider installing it on their main device. The pair also talks about ChatGPT vs Siri AI, OpenAI vs Apple, the end of carrier-financed unlocked iPhones, and nine upcoming new emoji.

  • Sponsored by Bitwarden: Check out Bitwarden Password Manager, featuring an Apple Watch authenticator integration, secure autofill on Safari and iOS apps, and enterprise-grade security tools that help you manage credentials with confidence.

items.title: Apple raises prices for Apple Music and Apple One subscriptions
items.summary:
Apple has raised the price of Apple Music today in the United States and other countries. Starting today, Apple Music’s Individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, up from $10.99.

Apple has also increased the price of select Apple One bundles.

In a statement to 9to5Mac, Apple said: “As a result of rising licensing costs, Apple Music is increasing its subscription price beginning today.”

items.title: OpenAI tweaks chat access in the ChatGPT app for Mac
items.summary: OpenAI has updated its recently redesigned ChatGPT app for Mac to make chats easier to access once again. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple in talks to settle DOJ antitrust lawsuit, per report
items.summary:
Back in 2024, the US Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against Apple, accusing the company of a “smartphone monopoly.”

Apple has voiced its opposition to the case many times over the last two years. Now, a Bloomberg report says the DOJ and Apple are in talks to settle the lawsuit.

items.title: Car Keys in Apple Wallet reportedly coming to major Chinese automaker
items.summary: As Apple continues expanding Car Keys support across the auto industry, the feature now appears to be headed to select vehicles from a major Chinese automaker. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple TV drops first trailer for ‘The Dynasty: UConn Huskies’
items.summary: After announcing ‘The Dynasty: UConn Huskies’ last May, Apple TV today released the first trailer for the docuseries, ahead of its debut next month. Watch it below.

items.title: WhatsApp starts activating reserved usernames for select iPhone users
items.summary: After rolling out a username reservation system a few days ago, WhatsApp is now beginning to activate the full feature for a limited number of users. Here are the details.

items.title: What will you do if Apple’s higher pricing turns out to be permanent? [Poll]
items.summary:
When Apple finally responded to the dramatically increased costs of memory and other components, the company didn’t hold back. Some of the price hikes exceeded 50%, while memory and storage upgrades literally doubled in some cases.

There’s a strong possibility that these higher prices will remain in effect for years, and quite possibly permanently …

items.title: Deals: AirPods Pro 3, Magic Keyboard all-time low, iPhone 17 Pro Max up to $449 off, ChargeKey tool, more
items.summary: We are kicking of Friday’s deals with the ongoing $50 price drop on AirPods Pro 3, the best price in months on AirPods 4 with ANC, and a chance to score them from $119, but we also have this Apple Watch Series 11 cell model at $130 off and Apple’s latest Magic Keyboard with Touch ID and Numeric Keypad still down at its best price ever. You can land iPhone 17 Pro Max at up to $449 off and this base M5 MacBook Pro 1TB with the 96W Apple charger upgrade at $170 off alongside Nomad’s USB-C keychain charging and data transfer tool and deals on almost all of the brand’s Apple Watch bands at up to 25% off. Check it out below.

items.title: The best CarPlay upgrades to try in the iOS 27 public beta
items.summary: The iOS 27 public beta is now available, and it includes several CarPlay changes worth checking out. Here’s what you should try after updating.

items.title: Apple overtakes Nvidia as world’s most valuable company as it barrels toward $5T
items.summary: Apple has officially reclaimed the world’s most valuable company title based on market cap. The company overtook Nvidia during intraday trading on Friday with its stock up 17 points this week.

items.title: 9to5Mac Daily: July 17, 2026 – Apple’s Back to School promo, AirPods public beta
items.summary:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.

items.title: Apple sends legal letters to former employees now at OpenAI
items.summary:
Last week, Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in which it alleged that its former employees have stolen trade secrets “for the benefit of OpenAI.”

As part of this process, Apple has reportedly sent letters directly to dozens of its former employees now working at OpenAI.

items.title: Apple’s M6 chip is coming soon, here’s everything we know
items.summary: Apple will reportedly launch its next-generation Apple Silicon chip, the M6, later this year. Here’s everything we know so far, including why this generation will be unprecedented in the Apple Silicon era.

items.title: Apple TV for Android adds widgets, Apple Music gets WhatsApp sharing
items.summary: Apple TV and Apple Music for Android are rolling out sizable updates today, with homescreen widgets as the shared theme.

items.title: iOS 27 adds an all-new app to your iPhone’s Home Screen
items.summary: Apple released the iOS 27 public beta this week, with a ton of new Siri and Apple Intelligence features. One of the biggest changes here is the addition of an all-new Siri app. This marks the first time that Siri has been pre-installed as a standalone app for iPhone users …

items.title: iOS 27 public betas are out, Siri AI hands on, and Apple sues OpenAI
items.summary:
Benjamin and Chance talk about their latest experiences with iOS 27 and Siri AI, with Apple releasing the public betas this week so anyone in the whole world can give the new software a go. The company also sued OpenAI this week in a high-profile case over alleged trade secrets theft. And, as is tradition, the Apple Store Back to School offer underwhelms.

And in Happy Hour Plus, Mayo gets a new work laptop which complicates his future Mac buying decisions. Subscribe at 9to5mac.com/join.

items.title: LM Studio expands beyond chat with Bionic, a new AI agent app for open models
items.summary: LM Studio is launching LM Studio Bionic, a new app for Mac and Windows that uses open models to handle coding, research, and complex work with documents and files. Here are the details.

items.title: Class action accuses Apple of misleading users about Hide My Email’s privacy protections
items.summary: A California Apple user has filed a proposed class action accusing the company of misleading customers about Hide My Email’s privacy protections. Here are the details.

items.title: iOS 27 makes one of my favorite Apple Music features even better
items.summary: Last year, Apple debuted a new feature called AutoMix in Apple Music. With this year’s iOS 27, the feature is getting a big upgrade with much better transitions than before.

items.title: Roblox adding game creation feature to iPhone and iPad app
items.summary: Roblox says it will soon bring game creation to mobile for the first time. This will allow users to create new playable experiences directly inside the iPhone and iPad app.

items.title: NotebookLM is now Gemini Notebook, with 3.5 + Antigravity upgrade coming to AI Pro
items.summary: Google announced today that NotebookLM is now called “Gemini Notebook” and previewed upcoming features that users can soon expect.

items.title: Apple TV sets release date for Stillwater’s fifth season
items.summary: Apple TV announced today that its Peabody and Emmy Award-winning mindfulness series Stillwater will return next month for a new season. Here are the details.

items.title: Deals: AirPods with ANC from $119, AirPods Pro 3, MacBook Pro up to $400 off, Thunderbolt 5 Dock, more
items.summary: Today’s 9to5Toys Lunch Break is headlined by the ongoing $50 price drop on AirPods Pro 3, but we also have AirPods 4 with ANC hitting best price in months at Amazon today, as well as a chance to land a set Apple Watch Series 11 lineup and a rare Amazon low on Hyper’s latest 120Gbps Thunderbolt 5 Dock with SSD enclosure $80 off. Head below for a closer look.

items.title: AppleCare One warranty bundle just became an even better value for customers
items.summary: While Apple’s latest AppleCare+ price increase for Mac and iPad is small in isolation, it makes AppleCare One an even better value. Apple increasing the price of Mac and iPad hardware also makes warranty coverage a stronger consideration.

items.title: Hands-on: Satechi’s new color-matched MacBook Neo accessories add ports and extra functionality
items.summary: A few months back, the MacBook Neo took the tech world by storm, delivering the best-value laptop of 2026. Even after the recent price increase, it remains the most recommended entry-level laptop for most people. And just like Apple’s other cost-effective products, they brought some playful colors into the mix, like Citrus, Blush, Indigo, and Silver, giving these MacBooks a pop of personality and color. But one gripe I had was that most MacBook accessories are color-matched to the traditional silver or space grey colors. That is where Satechi comes in with their new trio of color-matched accessories for the MacBook Neo. They add more ports, connectivity, and functionality without taking away from what makes the Neo visually unique. Here is what you should know!

items.title: Rumor: Apple increases orders for key component ahead of foldable iPhone launch
items.summary: Apple has reportedly ramped up orders for vapor chambers, signaling that the cooling technology could play a bigger role across its future hardware lineup. Here are the details.

items.title: Report: Apple targets October launch for OLED iPad mini, kicking off next wave of iPad updates
items.summary: entry-level iPad, the iPad Air, and the iPad Pro. Here are the details.

items.title: WhatsApp adds in-call controls for Voice Isolation and Wide Spectrum on iPhone
items.summary: WhatsApp is rolling out in-call settings that allow users to switch between the iPhone’s different Mic Modes. Here are the details.

items.title: Meta AI can now alert parents when their teens discuss self-harm
items.summary:
Meta has announced that parents can receive alerts when their teens discuss suicide or other forms of self-harm with its AI on Instagram, Facebook, or Meta Horizons.

You’ll need to opt in to Meta’s supervision tools for parents and guardians in order to select the family accounts you want to supervise …

items.title: 1Password now lets Claude sign in to websites without seeing your passwords
items.summary: 1Password is launching a new Claude integration for Mac users today. It’s designed to let Anthropic’s AI agent sign in to websites without seeing your password or two-factor authentication code.

items.title: Signal Ring gives blood pressure readings, not just alerts like Apple Watch
items.summary:
Five Apple Watch models are able to measure your blood pressure in order to generate alerts for possible hypertension. However, they do not provide your actual blood pressure readings.

A few smart rings and other fitness devices do so, but rely on regular calibration with a traditional cuff monitor. The upcoming Signal Ring says that it delivers full blood pressure measurements without any calibration, and that a trial of thousands of people backs the claim …

items.title: Windows games run better than ever on Macs, officially or unofficially
items.summary:
There are few software limitations on anyone considering a switch from Windows to Mac. Not only do all the biggest PC apps run on the platform, but there are countless examples of useful apps that are exclusive to Mac.

The one big exception is gaming. However, new tests show that Windows games run better than ever on Mac, whether that’s being done officially or unofficially …

items.title: TSMC says it may build 12 Arizona chip plants in total, but be skeptical
items.summary:
Apple chipmaker TSMC had already said that it would build a total of eight plants in Arizona, and has now announced an investment boost that could see that number increased to 12.

This could potentially see more chips for Apple devices made in the US, but there are reasons to be skeptical about this possibility …

items.title: 9to5Mac Daily: July 16, 2026 – OpenAI responds to Apple, more
items.summary:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.

items.title: Security Bite Podcast: Apple is watching your taps
items.summary:
9to5Mac Security Bite is exclusively brought to you by Mosyle, the only Apple Unified Platform. Making Apple devices work-ready and enterprise-safe is all we do. Our unique integrated approach to management and security combines state-of-the-art Apple-specific security solutions for fully automated Hardening & Compliance, Next Generation EDR, AI-powered Zero Trust, and exclusive Privilege Management with the most powerful and modern Apple MDM on the market. The result is a totally automated Apple Unified Platform currently trusted by over 45,000 organizations to make millions of Apple devices work-ready with no effort and at an affordable cost. Request your EXTENDED TRIAL today and understand why Mosyle is everything you need to work with Apple.

Talal from Mysk is back on Security Bite. We get into how and why the App Store logs every tap you make, why researchers are publicly walking away from Apple’s bug bounty program, and Loupe, Mysk’s new free and open-source app that shows you exactly what iOS’s APIs are sharing about you.

items.title: Apple Store launches 2026 Back to School offer: get up to $150 gift card with Mac or iPad purchase
items.summary:
Apple today launched its Back to School deal for 2026. Students can get a gift card worth up to $150 with the purchase of a MacBook Pro. $100 gift cards are available with the purchase of MacBook Air, iPad Pro, and iPad Air.

As is typical, you can usually save more money than the $150 gift card included in Apple’s promo by buying a new Mac from Amazon. However, it can still represent a reasonable deal for students who want to buy from the Apple Store directly.

items.title: AirPods just got a long-requested feature in new public beta
items.summary: While it was overshadowed by the iOS 27 public beta, Apple also released a new public beta firmware for AirPods this week. The update brings a long-requested feature to supported AirPods models: custom EQ.

items.title: I can’t work on my Mac without this little keypad anymore
items.summary: Offloading keyboard shortcuts to a programmable macro keypad is the best productivity hack I’ve ever done.

items.title: Apple faces new questions from Brazil over betting apps accessible to minors
items.summary: Nearly three months after Brazilian officials first questioned Apple over betting apps accessible to minors, the company has now been given five business days to provide additional details about its App Store safeguards. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple Maps won’t allow ads from these categories at launch
items.summary: Ads are coming to Apple Maps in the United States and Canada sometime this summer. Ahead of that launch, Apple has shared more details about what will and won’t be allowed for ads in Apple Maps.

items.title: AppleCare+ gets a price increase for new Mac and iPad plans
items.summary: As Bloomberg reports, Apple has increased its monthly and annual AppleCare+ rates for new Mac and iPad customers. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple wins discovery fight over federal agency documents in DOJ antitrust case
items.summary: A specially appointed judge has sided with Apple in its fight to obtain documents from 14 federal agencies as part of its defense against the DOJ’s antitrust lawsuit. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple highlights 2026 sales tax holidays for Macs, iPads, and more in 10 states
items.summary:
Apple published its 2026 sales tax holiday page this week, outlining when customers in 10 U.S. states can buy select products without paying state sales tax.

Customers can shop tax-free online or in-store, but the dates, devices, and price limits vary dramatically.

items.title: Apple marks the return of MLS with new Apple Maps guides, Apple Music playlists, more
items.summary: With the 2026 World Cup drawing to a close, Major League Soccer returns tomorrow on Apple TV, alongside several tie-in features in Apple Maps, Apple Sports, Apple Fitness+, and more. Here are the details.

items.title: OpenAI selling $230 Codex Micro hardware product
items.summary: Last month, OpenAI teased Codex-branded hardware. Today, the company has released its collaboration with Work Louder: a $230 Codex Micro keyboard product.

items.title: Apple Watch Series 12: Four rumored new features coming soon
items.summary: Among the many new products Apple has coming this fall is the Apple Watch Series 12. Here are four rumored new features coming to this year’s Apple Watch update.

items.title: Suno now lets iPhone users generate songs directly in iMessage
items.summary: Starting today, iPhone users who have the Suno app installed can generate songs from the Messages app using text or voice prompts. Here are the details.

items.title: Deals: Apple Watch Series 11 $130 off, AirPods, M5 MacBook Air, iPhone 17 Pro, HomeKit lamp, Bose, more
items.summary: Alongside the ongoing $50 price drop on AirPods Pro 3 and the up to $130 off Apple Watch Series 11, today’s 9to5Toys Lunch Break is headlined by Amazon re-stocking select M5 MacBook Air models at $150 off and the Amazon Renewed Premium iPhone 17 Pro models at $220 off today (plus more up to $449 off). We also have Eve’s wireless HomeKit orb lamp with Siri at 25% off, the first discount on the new Bose Lifestyle Ultra soundbar and speakers bundle at $250 off, and Nomad leather iPhone 17/Air cases up to 30% off. Head below for a closer look at all of those and much more.

items.title: Report: Apple looking into buying chip startups to strengthen its AI infrastructure
items.summary: The Information reports that Apple has been in talks with bankers and semiconductor startups as it explores acquisitions to strengthen its AI server capabilities. Here are the details.

items.title: Report: How an email mistake derailed talks between Apple and OpenAI ahead of the lawsuit
items.summary: NBC News reports that talks between Apple and OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft broke down after an outside lawyer for Apple mistakenly emailed the wrong OpenAI employee and confused their interactions. Here are the details.

items.summary:

items.title: Apple adds new 36-month financing options for iPad purchases
items.summary: Apple has added new financing options for cellular iPad purchases. As of today, you can buy cellular iPads directly from Apple with 36-month financing from AT&T and Verizon.

items.title: Apple TV reveals first look and return date for ‘Where’s Wanda?’ season two
items.summary: Apple TV today shared a first look at season two of the German dark comedy ‘Where’s Wanda?’ and confirmed its return date. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple just closed a popular workaround for buying an unlocked iPhone
items.summary: Apple and its carrier partners have closed a workaround that let shoppers buy an unlocked iPhone with carrier financing. The change appears to have gone into effect this month.

items.title: OpenAI’s hardware device may partly compete with AirPods Ultra – and lose
items.summary:
Jony Ive and Sam Altman made a big promise when they teased the launch of a completely new concept in AI hardware more than a year ago.

The most recent report suggests that OpenAI may technically be delivering on this promise, but could end up at least partly competing with an upcoming AirPods model. If so, I can easily see AirPods Ultra proving the more popular choice …

items.title: Spotify’s managed accounts are no longer limited to Premium subscribers
items.summary: Starting today, Spotify users in several countries can create a managed account for their child without a Premium subscription. Here are the details.

items.title: iOS 27 breaks 15 years of muscle memory on iPhone and iPad
items.summary:
Since iOS 5 in 2011, the iPhone and iPad have included Notification Center, a central place to find alerts from various apps in chronological order.

Starting with iOS 27, Apple is making a major change to how users open Notification Center on iPhone and iPad.

iOS 27 is currently in beta, and the behavior has changed slightly between the first two releases. However, the third developer beta and first public beta show this change will ship this fall.

items.title: PSA: Beware of fake Mac crash reports out to steal your passwords, crypto wallets, more
items.summary:
While macOS is generally very stable, there are times when an app will crash and your Mac will offer to send a diagnostic report to Apple.

A new form of Mac malware has been discovered, which creates fake versions of this form, requesting your Mac password as part of the data collection. If you comply, it will get access to a huge range of personal data, including your password managers and cryptocurrency wallets …

items.title: Apple reaches agreement with Chinese government on Apple Intelligence rollout
items.summary:
The new Siri and other Apple Intelligence features are currently only supported in the iOS 27 beta in English in a small number of countries, but Apple has promised that they will “quickly expand” to additional countries and languages.

China posed a particular challenge for the company as the law there only permits partnering with a Chinese AI company. A report today says that Apple has now reached agreement on this, and will be using two different models …

items.title: A sneak peek at nine new(ish) emoji on the way
items.summary:
Unicode’s emoji tsar Jennifer Daniel has given us a sneak peek at nine emoji set for release next year – though not all of them are entirely new.

She says the committee responsible for the selection of emoji is doing some tidying up to help avoid misunderstandings …

items.title: Limited series ‘Lucky’, starring Anya Taylor-Joy, premieres on Apple TV
items.summary: The first episodes of Lucky, Apple TV’s new limited series starring Anya Taylor-Joy (The Menu), are now streaming. Watch the trailer below.

items.title: iOS 27: My favorite new iPhone features (other than AI)
items.summary:
The iOS 27 public beta is here, and the headlining changes are Siri AI and the array of new Apple Intelligence features. However, the update also includes plenty of non-AI features.

Here are a few of my favorite iOS 27 features (other than Siri and AI).

items.title: Opera gains ground among iPhone users in the US and UK
items.summary: Opera says its iOS browser saw monthly active users grow 93% in the UK and 50% in the US during Q2 2026 compared with the same period last year. Here are the details.

items.title: Report: OpenAI’s first device will be a portable speaker with a camera and other sensors
items.summary: According to Bloomberg, OpenAI’s anticipated entry into the hardware market is slated to begin with a portable, screenless smart speaker. Here are the details.

items.title: OpenAI says it has seen no evidence supporting Apple’s trade secret theft claims
items.summary: OpenAI has issued a new statement in reaction to Apple’s lawsuit accusing the company of trade secret theft. Here are the details.

items.title: Judge dismisses lawsuit accusing Apple of failing to stop CSAM on iCloud
items.summary: Reuters reports that Apple has defeated a proposed class-action lawsuit accusing it of failing to stop child sexual abuse material from being stored and shared through iCloud. Here are the details.

items.title: Canva Code 2.0 adds visual editing, HTML imports, and real-time collaboration
items.summary: Canva today unveiled Canva Code 2.0, an AI-powered vibe coding platform that lets users build and visually edit websites, apps, and interactive experiences. Here are the details.

items.title: AirPods public betas arrive one day after iOS 27 public beta, here’s how to enroll
items.summary: Following yesterday’s release of the AirPods Pro 3, AirPods Pro 2, and AirPods (4th gen). Here are the details.

items.title: PrismML releases Bonsai 27B, claiming first major AI model of its size fit for iPhone
items.summary: PrismML is back in the news today after the AI startup’s CEO told CNBC that Apple is looking at the company’s technology. The company has also released its Bonsai 27B model that it says runs on iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

items.title: Apple Watch among wearables exempted from EU user-replaceable battery rules
items.summary: The European Commission has revised its battery regulations to exempt devices such as the Apple Watch from requirements that batteries be removable and replaceable by users. Here are the details.

items.title: Your iPhone might miss out on these iOS 27 features
items.summary: The iOS 27 public beta is now available, allowing iPhone users to try out the new features the update offers. Which iPhones are compatible with iOS 27? Here’s everything you need to know.

items.title: MacWhisper 14 launches with a new transcript editor, faster performance, more
items.summary: One of the best AI-powered transcription apps for the Mac just got even better, with a major update that introduces a redesigned transcript editor and significant performance improvements. Here are the details.

items.title: Spotify now lets Premium users talk to a chatbot about music, podcasts, and books
items.summary: Spotify will now let subscribers use natural language to ask questions about their listening history, make playback requests, and more. Here are the details.

items.title: 9to5Mac Daily: July 14, 2026 – M7 Ultra rumors, iPhone 18 costs
items.summary:

items.title: Deals: AirPods Pro 3, Nomad 25W MagSafe stands, Find My cards, titanium/leather Apple Watch bands, more
items.summary: Alongside the ongoing $50 price drop on AirPods Pro 3, today’s 9to5Toys Lunch Break is headlined by the slate Titanium 46mm Apple Watch Series 11 at $184 off, $130 off the aluminum Cell model, and more from $299. The rare Apple keyboards and black Magic Mouse deals are (somehow) still live, and we have a solid discount on my personal favorite Anker Prime Power Bank that doubles as wall charger, but today’s real accessory highlight is Nomad’s giant sitewide Anniversary sale with rare deals on MagSafe chargers, cases, Find My cards, Watch bands, more. Head below for a closer look at the details.

items.title: Leaker says next iPad mini will stick with a 60Hz display despite OLED upgrade
items.summary: Apple’s next iPad mini might miss out on ProMotion despite switching to OLED, per leaker yeux1122. Here are the details.

items.title: Anthropic is giving teachers free access to premium Claude features, details here
items.summary: Anthropic is providing free access to premium Claude AI features to K-12 teachers in the United States. The effort is part of a new Claude for Teachers initiative.

items.title: IDC: Apple shipped nearly 1 in 5 smartphones in China in Q2 2026
items.summary: In a report published today, IDC said Huawei and Apple were the only smartphone makers to record shipment growth in China during the second quarter of 2026. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple Arcade adding the ultimate NFL game just in time for football season
items.summary:
Apple Arcade is scoring a major hit game in August. Apple announced today that the subscription game service will add Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition on August 6. The new game will arrive just before the new NFL pre-season kicks off.

Apple says the new Madden NFL 27 Arcade Edition title is designed just for Apple Arcade. Apple Arcade games have no ads and no in-app purchases.

items.title: WhatsApp is building its own iCloud alternative for chat backups
items.summary: References found in the latest TestFlight build of WhatsApp indicate that Meta is working on bringing an alternative to iCloud backups for iPhone users. Here are the details.

items.title: Apple’s MacBook Pro overhaul is coming soon, with a big twist
items.summary: Apple just released a MacBook Pro update in March, but there’s more coming soon. Apple is expected to debut one of its biggest MacBook Pro updates ever as soon as later this year. Here’s everything we know so far … including details on a surprising recent twist.

items.title: Apple says Epic’s arguments against pausing the case are ‘wrong’
items.summary: Apple has responded to Epic’s opposition to its request to pause lower-court proceedings over App Store commissions while the Supreme Court reviews part of the case. Here’s what that means.

items.title: How the 20th anniversary iPhone will provide a ‘single slab of glass’ look
items.summary:
Former Apple design chief Jony Ive always described his long-term vision for the iPhone design as “a single slab of glass.” Apple is believed to have retained that goal long after Ive left the company, with Liquid Glass representing the company’s software prep ahead of the new hardware.

We’ve already seen reports of a number of ways in which the 20th anniversary iPhone could take us significantly closer to Apple’s vision, and the latest of these may provide another piece of the puzzle …

items.title: Apple TV’s next action-comedy starring Ryan Reynolds gets first trailer
items.summary: Apple TV today released the first trailer for Mayday, an action comedy starring Ryan Reynolds (Deadpool) and Kenneth Branagh (Oppenheimer). Watch it below.

items.title: Top watchOS 27 features that will enhance your Apple Watch
items.summary: watchOS 27 packs some great new features. Here’s everything new coming to Apple Watch this fall so far.

items.title: Your X feed might now work, as platform finally does what everyone wanted
items.summary: Many X users have found that their feed is rather random, with comments on their posts similarly more from randos than those who follow you. The platform says it has finally noticed this and is fixing it with a “tweak” to the algorithm …

items.title: Waze now learns your preferences, and gets more conversational but less chatty
items.summary:
Navigation app Waze is rolling out an update to its iPhone and Android app to introduce three new AI-powered features.

The first uses your journey history to understand your routing preferences in order to provide personalized navigation options …

items.title: Safety regulator says Apple needs to do more to combat sextortion
items.summary:
An online safety regulator has said that tech giants need to do more to combat sextortion (sexual extortion), naming Apple, Meta and Google among the companies who need to do better.

Victims of sexual blackmail tend to be younger, including children below the age of 16 …

items.title: Apple @ Work Podcast: Breaking down the recent IDC PC shipment report
items.summary:
Apple @ Work is exclusively brought to you by Mosyle, the only Apple Unified Platform. Mosyle is the only solution that integrates in a single professional-grade platform all the solutions necessary to seamlessly and automatically deploy, manage & protect Apple devices at work. Over 45,000 organizations trust Mosyle to make millions of Apple devices work-ready with no effort and at an affordable cost. Request your EXTENDED TRIAL today and understand why Mosyle is everything you need to work with Apple.

In this episode of Apple @ Work, Jitesh Ubrani joins the show to discuss IDC’s recent PC shipment report, where shipments declined, but revenue rose.

items.title: iOS 27 public beta: Should you install it on your iPhone?
items.summary: The iOS 27 public beta is now available, and it’s full of major new features. Should you install it on your iPhone? Here’s everything you need to know.

items.title: OpenAI could force Apple into an awkward fight with Jony Ive
items.summary: While Apple did its best to insulate Jony Ive in its lawsuit against OpenAI, the company may not be able to keep its former design chief out of the fight. Here’s why.

items.title: Second report says Apple posted its strongest-ever Q2 for iPhone shipments amid market downturn
items.summary: Adding to an earlier report from Counterpoint Research that showed Apple posting its strongest-ever second quarter for iPhone shipments, Omdia has now published its own figures pointing to the same record performance. Here are the details.

items.title: 9to5Mac Daily: July 13, 2026 – Apple sues OpenAI
items.summary:
Listen to a recap of the top stories of the day from 9to5Mac. 9to5Mac Daily is available on iTunes and Apple’s Podcasts app, Stitcher, TuneIn, Google Play, or through our dedicated RSS feed for Overcast and other podcast players.

items.title: tvOS 27 and HomePod 27 public betas now available, here’s what’s new
items.summary: Apple has just released tvOS 27 in public beta, so any Apple TV 4K user can install the software in advance of its fall launch. HomePod 27 is available in public beta too. Here’s what’s new.
